title: Midterm Exam
description:
1. The exam is in-person, during our regular class time

2. The exam is closed notes/books and no pre-populated Excel spreadsheets are allowed

3. You are allowed to use a printed formula sheet (front and back)

4. Please save your Excel work and your hand-written notes

5. Please upload your work (Excel work and pictures of any hand-written work) by going to Midterm Exam Work Upload on Canvas within 20 minutes after the test ended.

6. Please note: You must have supporting work to receive full credit for a question.
descriptionText:
1. The exam is in-person, during our regular class time
2. The exam is closed notes/books and no pre-populated Excel spreadsheets are allowed
3. You are allowed to use a printed formula sheet (front and back)
4. Please save your Excel work and your hand-written notes
5. Please upload your work (Excel work and pictures of any hand-written work) by going to Midterm Exam Work Upload on Canvas within 20 minutes after the test ended.
6. Please note: You must have supporting work to receive full credit for a question.

questions.text:
Consider the following monthly return data on 4 stocks and the S&P500: data_midterm.xlsx
    

Download data_midterm.xlsx
Suppose also that the risk-free rate is 0.0012.
 
Questions
 
Question 1: Suppose that on January 1, 2003, you invested in a portfolio P that allocated 25% in Ford, 10% in GM, 35% in WMT,  20% in TGT, and the rest in S&P500. You balanced this portfolio at the beginning of each month until July 30, 2021. What was the Sharpe ratio of this portfolio over the entire sample period (2003/01-2021/07)?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = 12.6%
B = 10.94%
C = 11.45%
D = 13.14%
E = 14.49%
 
Question 2: Suppose that on January 1, 2023, you invested $1 million in portfolio P from part 1. Assuming that rebalanced your portfolio every month until July 30, 2021, how much should your portfolio be worth on July 30, 2021?
 
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $5,113,804
B = $4,997,004
C = $4,997,596
D = $5,306,763
E = $4,854,995
 
Question 3: Suppose that on January 1, 2023, you invested $1 million in portfolio P from part 1, and then decided to never rebalance until July 30, 2021. How much should your portfolio be worth on July 30, 2021?
 
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $5,113,804
B = $4,997,004
C = $4,997,596
D = $5,306,763
E = $4,854,995
 
Question 4: What is the Sharpe's ratio of the unbalanced portfolio from part 3 over the entire sample period (2003/01-2021/07)?
 
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = 12.6%
B = 10.94%
C = 11.45%
D = 13.14%
E = 14.49%

questions.text:
You work in the wealth management group at Well Fargo. A client is interested in building a portfolio that has exposure to domestic equities, international equities and gold. You did some research and came up with 3 ETFs: SPY for domestic equities, IEFA for international equities, and GLD for gold.
 
Below are some statistics for the annual returns of these ETFs:




SPY
IEFA
GLD


Expected return
11%
12%
7%


Return volatility
21%
25%
17%



 
The covariance between SPY and IEFA is 0.05, the covariance between SPY and GLD is -0.01, and the covariance between IEFA and GLD is -0.015.
 
In addition, the 1-year T-Bills yield (i.e., the risk-free rate) is 2.8%.
 
Questions 
 
Question 1: Suppose you want to build a portfolio that allocates 45% in SPY, 35% in IEFA and the rest in GLD. What is the Sharpe's ratio of this portfolio?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 31.2%
B = 45.05%
C = 39.72%
D = 54.82%
E = 49.47%
 
Question 2: You want to build the optimal risky portfolio generated by SPY, IEFA and GLD. What is the Sharpe's ratio of this portfolio?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 31.2%
B = 45.05%
C = 39.72%
D = 54.82%
E = 49.47%
 
Question 3: To reduce the risk of your client's portfolio, you plan to combine the optimal risky portfolio in Part 2 with the T-Bills. How much weight should you put on T-Bills such that the portfolio of the optimal risky portfolio and the T-Bills has a return volatility of 10%?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 10.48%
B = 12.6%
C = 16.93%
D = 8.52%
E = 14.28%
 
Question 4: Suppose your client has a risk aversion of A = 5. You are building the optimal (complete) portfolio for your client, that combines the optimal risky portfolio in Part 2 and the T-Bills. What is the allocation of GLD in your client's complete portfolio?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 49.3%
B = 18.1%
C = 27.8%
D = 31.8%
E = 76.2%

questions.text:
You work for Amundi Funds out of Paris, and your boss asked you to set up a strategy that buys long the ETF SPY (domestic stocks) and short sales the ETF IEFA (international stocks). PNB Paribas is Amundi's broker and have the following margin requirements:
1) Initial margin for buying on margin and for short selling is 65%
2) Maintenance margin on buying on margin is 35%
3) Maintenance margin on short-selling is 50%
4) Interest rate on margin loans is 3% per year
 
The strategy requires that you buy long 10,000 shares of SPY and sell short 60,000 shares of IEFA.
 
Suppose that today is Aug 20, 2025, and that a shares of SPY trades at $650, while a share of IEFA trades at $110.
 
Questions
 
Question 1: Suppose that on Oct 20, 2025 (two months later), a share of SPY trades for $610. What should be the percentage equity of your SPY position  in your margin account?
 
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 61.59%
B = 51.25%
C = 62.52%
D = 46.8%
E = 57.36%
 
Question 2: Suppose that on Oct 20, 2025 (two months later), a share of IEFA trades for $105. What should be the percentage equity of your IEFA position in your margin account?
 
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 67.58%
B = 59.61%
C = 62.52%
D = 72.86%
E = 80.26%
 
Question 3: What is the lowest price that a share of IEFA should be trading on Oct 20, 2025 (two months later) for the Amundi to get a margin call on the IEFA position?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = $121
B = $134.44
C = $110
D = $127.6
E = $118.52
 
Question 4: Suppose that on Oct 20, 2025 (two months later), a share of SPY trades for $610 while a share of IEFA trades for $105. What is the return on your strategy between Aug 20, 2025 and Oct 20, 2025?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D and E
A = 6.21%
B = 3.8%
C = 0.27%
D = -4.03%
E = -5.21%
